title: Tavion Axmis
content:

{{Quote|You're delusional! Kyle told me all about you, how you went crawling away after he defeated you!|[[Jaden Korr]], to Tavion Axmis|audio=JadenCallsTavionDelusional.flac|<ref name="Jedi Academy" />}}
'''Tavion Axmis''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who was at large during the early years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Apprenticed under the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Desann]], Axmis traveled with him to find the lost [[Valley of the Jedi]] and served alongside him in [[Hethrir]]'s [[Empire Reborn]]. After her [[Battle of Bespin (Empire Reborn)|defeat]] at the hands of [[Kyle Katarn]] and Desann's [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Empire Reborn)|subsequent death]], she became the leader of the [[Disciples of Ragnos]] cult. She took [[Alora (Dark Jedi)|Alora]] as her apprentice, and set herself upon the task of resurrecting the ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Marka Ragnos]].

During [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] search for the cult, she turned [[Rosh Penin]], one of Katarn's [[Padawan/Legends|apprentices]] and a student at the [[Jedi Praxeum]], to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Katarn and his apprentice [[Jaden Korr]] encountered and confronted her, and Penin at [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]]. Following this, Axmis journeyed to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] tomb [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] to resurrect Ragnos; Korr was sent to [[Battle of Korriban (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|stop her]], and he defeated her after a fierce [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]]. In desperation, Axmis let the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Ragnos fill her, and he guided her body in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]]. She was [[Death/Legends|killed]] after Korr defeated Ragnos, her body unable to withstand the ordeal.

==Biography==
===Desann's apprentice===
{{Quote|Where's your master, apprentice?|Kyle Katarn, to Tavion Axmis|audio=KyleTauntsTavion.flac|<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" />}}
[[File:Taviondesann.jpg|thumb|left|221x221px|Axmis and [[Desann]], traveling to the [[Valley of the Jedi]].]]
Originally an [[Empire Youth]] in [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Hethrir]]'s [[Empire Reborn]] movement, Tavion Axmis displayed signs of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] and was "purified" by the extra-dimensional being [[Waru]]. She received training in the use of her [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] under [[Desann]], Hethrir's right hand and failed [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] trainee; during this training, Axmis was selected by Desann to be his personal apprentice,<ref name="DFS6">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}}</ref> and the two conceived of a scheme to discover the location of the lost [[Valley of the Jedi]]. During the [[Battle of Artus Prime]], Desann and Axmis intercepted [[Jan Ors]], the partner of [[Kyle Katarn]] and one of the only two people to know the location of the Valley. Axmis managed to capture her and bring her aboard their shuttle. Katarn arrived to rescue his partner, only to be quickly defeated by Desann. On Desann's order Axmis, in the shuttle and out of Katarn's line of sight, activated her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and appeared to kill Ors. Desann spared Katarn and boarded his shuttle, leaving [[Artus Prime|the planet]] with Axmis.<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" />

However, they did not leave the system. When Katarn left in the ''[[Raven's Claw]]'', he immediately went to the Valley of the Jedi to regain his former Force-sensitivity, with the intention of using it to exact revenge on Ors's killers. Desann and Axmis followed him there; as Desann piloted their ship to the planet's surface, Axmis contacted [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Galak Fyyar]] to alert him of the Valley's location. Soon after, Axmis and Desann brought the presumed-dead Jan Ors to Admiral Fyyar's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Doomgiver]]''. After building up a deadly new force known as the [[Reborn]], Axmis left her master's side to oversee [[Imperial Remnant]] forces on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] who were smuggling [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] for the [[shadowtrooper]]s.<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" />

[[File:Apprenticeofdesann.jpg|thumb|184x184px|Axmis, on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], acting on behalf of Desann.]]
While on Bespin, Axmis confronted Kyle Katarn, whose search for Desann had led him to [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. She taunted him, announcing that they had only killed Ors to trick him into leading them to the Valley, and congratulated him for beginning the "era of Desann." After an intense [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|duel]] on one of Cloud City's landing platforms, Axmis was overcome by Katarn's superior skill with a lightsaber. Angry over Ors's apparent death, Katarn [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Choke|held Axmis aloft with]] [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] over the platform's edge. Axmis begged for her life, saying that she was "not brave enough to die", and revealed to Katarn that Ors was alive on board the ''Doomgiver'', which was docked at a [[Cairn Installation|base]] in the [[Lenico Belt]]. Katarn spared her, commanding her to get away before Desann found her.<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" />

After obeying Katarn's order and escaping, Axmis lost her master, who was slain by Katarn in the underground of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" />

===Cult leader===
{{Quote|You should have killed me when you had the chance, Katarn. Your pathetic Jedi mercy is a weakness that will cost you your life, and the life of your academy's students.|Tavion Axmis, after revealing herself to [[Kyle Katarn]]|<ref name="Jedi Academy" />}}
After Desann's death, Axmis took on an eager and sadistic apprentice: the [[Lethan/Legends|Lethan]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Alora (Dark Jedi)|Alora]]. She then acquired an ancient artifact known as the [[Scepter of Ragnos]] from a merchant on [[Commenor/Legends|Commenor]], who did not survive their meeting. With the Scepter, she drained [[Vergence/Legends|areas]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] strong with the Force, and empowered her [[Sith cultist/Legends|new followers]], creating the Disciples of Ragnos, who sought to resurrect the ancient [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] known as Marka Ragnos. She invited surviving Reborn to join her, which became her [[New Reborn]].<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

[[File:TAVION.jpg|thumb|left|205x205px|Axmis and [[Rosh Penin]], in [[Bast Castle]].]]
Eventually Axmis made a bold move on Yavin 4, [[Yavin 4 Incursion|deploying]] Alora to infiltrate the Jedi Academy to access Luke Skywalker's private record of the locations of Force nexuses, while Axmis and the [[Kothos twins]] drained the Force energy at a [[Unidentified Massassi temple|Massassi Temple]]. To avoid interference, the energy of the scepter was harnessed to shoot down an arriving shuttle carrying a load of new [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]]. However, Axmis's actions did not go unnoticed; Jaden Korr, a newly arrived Jedi Padawan, made his way to the Temple after being separated from the main group of Jedi on the arriving starship. He spotted Axmis's activity, forcing her to knock him out with the energy of the scepter. Despite Korr's intervention, Axmis and her forces escaped Yavin 4 safely with the valuable data.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Soon, Luke Skywalker dispatched the Jedi to areas of strong Force energy in an attempt to intercept the cult. One of these students, Rosh Penin, was captured by Axmis [[Mission to Byss (Rosh Penin)|on his mission]] to [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. She used his fear of being killed against him, and turned Penin to the dark side.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Axmis and Penin traveled to Vjun, the location of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Bast Castle]], and were attempting to drain the castle's Force energy when Jaden Korr arrived. Axmis remained in the shadows as the two former friends [[Mission to Vjun (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|dueled]]. Axmis revealed herself after Korr defeated Penin and Kyle Katarn arrived. She refused to give up her new apprentice, and told Katarn to save his pity because of the shame he caused her on Bespin. Axmis ripped Korr's lightsaber from his grasp with the Force and threw it to weaken the ceiling, which she then brought down on the Jedi with the power of the Scepter of Ragnos. The ensuing chaos allowed her to escape with Penin.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

[[File:Tavionragnos.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Axmis's body, possessed by [[Marka Ragnos]].]]
After [[Mission to Taspir III|neutralizing]] the cult's activities on Taspir III, Korr killed Alora and saved Penin, who was redeemed from the darkness. Korr and a team of Jedi then went to Korriban to stop Axmis from resurrecting Marka Ragnos, culminating in an epic battle between the Jedi and the Disciples of Ragnos.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Korr made his way to the [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos]] and confronted Axmis, eventually defeating her; but she refused to cower as she did before Katarn. She took the Scepter of Ragnos and released all of the Scepter's remaining energy into the ancient Sith Lord's remains. Ragnos's spirit rose, infusing Axmis's body with his strength, and dueled Korr again using Ragnos's ancient [[Sith sword]]. Despite Axmis fighting with Ragnos's enhanced dark side abilities; Korr was able to destroy the Scepter, forcing Ragnos's spirit to flee back into his tomb. His departure from Axmis's body killed her, and all the energy the New Reborn had stolen was returned to its proper place in the galaxy. When Jaden exited from the ancient structure, he permanently sealed it shut by placing boulders in front of the exit, forever sealing Tavion's husk of a body inside the evil tomb.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Silence, whelp. I will make Kyle Katarn's failure complete. My victory, Rosh's corruption, and now, your death will drive him to the dark side.|Tavion Axmis, to Jaden Korr|<ref name="Jedi Academy" />}}
Axmis was a powerful Dark Jedi skilled at lightsaber combat with occasional bouts of overconfidence masking an essential cowardice, exemplified by her begging for her life when she was defeated by Katarn.<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" /> After the death of Desann, Axmis ably led the Disciples of Ragnos, training her apprentice Alora to exhibit her own ruthlessness. However, her overconfidence led to her death: in her duel with Jaden Korr, she tried to use Ragnos's spirit to kill the young Jedi, believing his death and Penin's fall to the dark side would make Katarn's efforts a failure, turning Katarn to the dark side. Instead, her overconfidence failed her, and she died when Ragnos's spirit withdrew from her body.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Axmis's lightsaber was named "Stinger."<ref name="Note">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'': noted in the .sab file</ref>

==Powers and abilities==
Axmis was an extremely lethal enemy. She possessed incredible skill with the lightsaber and was a master of a variation of the [[Fast style]] of saber combat, which she would later teach to her apprentice Alora. Axmis would typically utilize flashy acrobatics with her sword technique, constantly flipping and somersaulting around her opponent, attacking them from many different angles in an attempt to overwhelm their defenses with her lightning-quick strikes.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

She was also very powerful in the Force. She was capable of a strong [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Push|Force push]], and possessed a high level of skill with the abilities of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] and [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Grip|Force grip]].<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" /><ref name="Jedi Academy" />

==Behind the scenes==
Axmis was voiced by [[Kath Soucie]] in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''<ref name="Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast" /> and ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Although her last name is never stated in the games, Axmis is the surname written in the [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="Board">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=219272&start=0#10841249|thread=Dark Jedi in the EU…|author=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-05-18|time=2:22 PM}}</ref>

===Axmis's homeworld===
Although there is no clear evidence, it is possible that Axmis may have been one of the [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]]—dark side witches from [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]], some of whom escaped the remote world despite its Imperial blockade. Both Axmis's style of clothing and her facial [[Tattoo/Legends|tattoo]] could be marks of a possible Dathomiri ancestry. Additionally, Luke Skywalker heard rumors about her when on a recruitment mission to Dathomir.<ref name="N.R.I. Reports">{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/spin.html|text=N.R.I. Reports|int=N.R.I. Reports}}</ref>

===Alternate end===
A bonus mission is included in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' (the Pit) in which Kyle Katarn—armed only with a lightsaber and his [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]]—faces off against Axmis. This is non-canon, since she is eventually killed by Jaden Korr on Korriban. The map itself made an appearance in the Multiplayer section of the game as a Duel map.

If the player chooses to turn to the dark side then, instead of resurrecting Marka Ragnos in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'', Axmis lets Jaden Korr kill her (seemingly unafraid of death anymore). After her death, Korr duels his master, Kyle Katarn.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />
